title: IMU CET Admit Card 2026 Out

detail.title: IMU CET Admit Card 2026 Out
detail.short_info: IMU CET Admit Card 2026 has been released on 16 May 2026 for the 24 May CBT exam. Download your IMU CET 2026 hall ticket from imu.edu.in / imu.cbexams.com using User ID and password, and check exam shift, timing, test city and centre details here.

content.text: Indian Maritime University (IMU) has released the IMU CET Admit Card 2026 for the Indian Maritime University Common Entrance Test on its official admission portal. The hall ticket is now available online for all registered candidates seeking admission to UG, PG and DNS programmes such as B.Tech Marine Engineering, B.Sc Nautical Science, DNS, MBA and M.Tech courses.

As per the official schedule, the IMU CET 2026 exam will be conducted on 24 May 2026 in computer‑based test (CBT) mode at exam centres across India. IMU has activated the admit card download link on 16 May 2026, and candidates can download their hall tickets up to the exam date.
Direct Link to Download IMU CET Admit Card 2026 Here

links.text: Direct Link to Download IMU CET Admit Card 2026 Here

sections.heading: IMU CET Admit Card 2026 - Key Details

headers: Indian Maritime University Common Entrance Test (IMU CET) 2026
rows: UG (B.Tech Marine Engg, B.Sc Nautical Science, BBA, DNS), PG (MBA, M.Tech) and other maritime programmes
User ID / Application Number and Password, plus Captcha/Security Code if prompted

sections.heading: How to Download IMU CET Admit Card 2026

items: Visit the official IMU website: https://www.imu.edu.in.

On the homepage, go to the “Admissions 2026-27” section and click on the “IMU CET 2026 Admit Card” link, which redirects to the admit card portal (imu.cbexams.com / apply.registernow.in/IMUCET/Admitcard).
On the admit card login page, enter your User ID / Application Number and Password and then the captcha/security code if shown.
Click on “Login / Submit” to access your dashboard.
Click on “Download Admit Card / Hall Ticket”; your IMU CET Admit Card 2026 will appear on the screen showing exam date, shift timing and exam centre address.
Verify all details carefully, then download and print 2-3 clear A4‑size copies of the hall ticket for exam day.

content.text: If you do not remember your login credentials, use the “I don’t remember my login details / Forgot password” option on the admit card portal and follow the steps sent to your registered email/mobile.

sections.heading: Details Mentioned on IMU CET Admit Card 2026

content.text: The IMU CET 2026 hall ticket contains the following key information, which candidates must verify:

items: Candidate’s name, photograph and signature
User ID / application number and IMU CET roll number
Exam name: IMU CET 2026
Course/category applied for (UG/PG, DNS, etc.) as per application
Exam date: 24 May 2026 and exam shift timing (for example, 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM)
Exam centre name, centre code and full address
Reporting time and gate‑closing time
Important exam‑day instructions, including items permitted and prohibited in the exam hall

content.text: If there is any discrepancy in personal details, photograph, course, or exam centre, candidates should immediately contact IMU via the helpline given in the admission notification.

sections.heading: Documents Required with IMU CET Admit Card 2026

content.text: On the day of the IMU CET 2026 exam, candidates should carry:

items: A printed copy of the IMU CET Admit Card 2026 (clear A4‑size printout; colour or good‑quality black and white).
One original valid photo ID proof, such as: Aadhaar Card PAN Card Passport Voter ID Driving Licence Any other government‑issued ID accepted as per IMU/centre instructions.
Aadhaar Card
PAN Card
Passport
Voter ID
Driving Licence
Any other government‑issued ID accepted as per IMU/centre instructions.
Additional documents if specified in the information brochure/admit card (for example, PwD certificate and scribe permission letter for candidates availing a scribe facility).

content.text: Entry to the exam centre will not be allowed without the printed IMU CET admit card and a valid photo ID.

sections.heading: Exam‑Day Instructions for IMU CET Admit Card 2026

content.text: To ensure smooth conduct of the IMU CET 2026 examination, candidates should follow these guidelines:

items: Reach the exam centre well before the reporting time mentioned on the IMU CET Admit Card 2026 so that verification, biometric/ID checks and seating can be completed; late entry after the gate‑closing time may not be allowed.

Carry only essential items: your IMU CET admit card 2026 (clear A4‑size printout), a valid photo ID (Aadhaar, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport or other government‑approved ID) and permitted stationery as specified in the IMU CET instructions (such as blue/black ball‑point pen if required; follow the CBT instructions carefully).

Do not carry mobile phones, smart watches, calculators (if not permitted), Bluetooth devices, earphones, notes, books or any other electronic gadgets or written material inside the examination hall; such items are strictly prohibited and may lead to cancellation of your paper and further disciplinary action.

Follow all instructions printed on the IMU CET admit card and those announced by the invigilators during the exam; indulging in any unfair means, talking to other candidates, or disobeying exam rules can result in cancellation of your examination and possible debarment from future tests.

Keep your IMU CET admit card safely even after the exam, as it may be required during counselling, seat allotment, document verification and admission formalities at Indian Maritime University and its campuses/affiliated institutes.

seo_tags: Indian Maritime University CET Admit Card
